# Review: deepmind/594.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` (line 17).
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (line 19).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 594`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/594](https://www.erdosproblems.com/594)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
- [x] Bibliography entries for related papers: [ErHa66], [Er69b], [EHS74].

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos594 ... end Erdos594`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos594`.
- [x] `open` statement appears **before** the namespace block: `open SimpleGraph Cardinal` (line 39).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `HasUncountableChromaticNumber` has a docstring (line 43ÔÇô44).
- [x] `cycleGraph` has a docstring (line 48ÔÇô49).
- [x] `SimpleGraph.ContainsCopy` has a docstring (line 55).
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed ÔÇö all definitions are either Props or constructive.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `HasUncountableChromaticNumber`, `ContainsCopy`.
- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase`: `cycleGraph`.
- [x] No problem-specific notation needed; none used.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions. *(Optional ÔÇö "consider" item, not required.)*

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_594` (snake_case).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same** attribute line: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` (line 70).
- [x] Has a docstring (lines 60ÔÇô68).
- [x] Docstring references source papers: [ErHa66], [Er69b], [EHS74].

### `answer()` usage

- [ ] **FAIL.** The original problem is phrased as a yes/no question: *"Does every graph with chromatic number Ôëą ÔäÁÔéü contain all sufficiently large odd cycles?"* (starts with "Does ÔÇŽ"). Per the checklist ┬ž6 Case 1, the theorem should use the `answer() Ôćö P` form. Since the problem has been solved affirmatively (proved by EHS74), it should be `answer(True) Ôćö P`.

  **Current** (Case 3 ÔÇö direct assertion):
  ```lean
  theorem erdos_594 {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V)
      (hG : HasUncountableChromaticNumber G) :
      Ôłâ NÔéÇ : ÔäĽ, ÔłÇ (n : ÔäĽ) (hn : n Ôëą 3), Odd n Ôćĺ n Ôëą NÔéÇ Ôćĺ
        G.ContainsCopy (cycleGraph n hn) := by
    sorry
  ```

  **Expected** (Case 1 ÔÇö yes/no question, solved affirmatively):
  ```lean
  theorem erdos_594 : answer(True) Ôćö
      ÔłÇ {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V),
        HasUncountableChromaticNumber G Ôćĺ
        Ôłâ NÔéÇ : ÔäĽ, ÔłÇ (n : ÔäĽ) (hn : n Ôëą 3), Odd n Ôćĺ n Ôëą NÔéÇ Ôćĺ
          G.ContainsCopy (cycleGraph n hn) := by
    sorry
  ```

- [ ] **FAIL.** Quantifiers `{V : Type*}`, `(G : SimpleGraph V)`, and `(hG : HasUncountableChromaticNumber G)` are currently theorem parameters. Per checklist ┬ž6: *"Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters."* They must be moved to the RHS of the `Ôćö`.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` ÔÇö valid.
- [x] Not `research open`, so the sorry-in-proof-body check is N/A.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number).
- [x] No duplicates.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Combinatorics). Appropriate for a graph chromatic number problem.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by sorry` (multi-line form with `sorry` on next line ÔÇö acceptable).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A ÔÇö no variants present. Single-part problem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ÔłÇ`, `Ôłâ`, `Ôëą`, `Ôćĺ`, `ÔäĽ`, `┬Č`, `Ôłž`, `ÔłĘ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (<100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` ÔÇö not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linters ÔÇö not verified in this review.

---

## Summary

**Status: NEEDS REVISION**

The formalization is well-structured with good docstrings, correct namespace conventions, appropriate AMS classification, and clean code quality. Three custom definitions (`HasUncountableChromaticNumber`, `cycleGraph`, `SimpleGraph.ContainsCopy`) are well-documented and correctly typed.

**One substantive issue must be fixed:**

The original problem is a yes/no question ("Does every graph ÔÇŽ"), so per the checklist it requires the `answer(True) Ôćö P` form (Case 1), not a bare assertion (Case 3). Additionally, the universal quantifiers currently appearing as theorem parameters must be moved inside the RHS of the `Ôćö` to comply with the `answer()` quantifier rule. Both issues are fixed by the rewrite shown above in ┬ž6.

    19Ôćĺ/-!
    20Ôćĺ# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 594
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/594](https://www.erdosproblems.com/594)
    23Ôćĺ
    24ÔćĺA problem of Erd┼Ĺs and Hajnal: does every graph with chromatic number
    25Ôćĺ$\geq \aleph_1$ contain all sufficiently large odd cycles? Proved by
    26ÔćĺErd┼Ĺs, Hajnal, and Shelah [EHS74].
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ[ErHa66] Erd┼Ĺs, P. and Hajnal, A., *On chromatic number of graphs and
    29Ôćĺset-systems*, Acta Math. Acad. Sci. Hung. **17** (1966), 61ÔÇô99.
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ[Er69b] Erd┼Ĺs, P., *Problems and results in combinatorial analysis and
    32Ôćĺgraph theory*, Proof Techniques in Graph Theory (1969), 27ÔÇô35.
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ[EHS74] Erd┼Ĺs, P., Hajnal, A., and Shelah, S., *On some general properties
    35Ôćĺof chromatic numbers*, Topics in Topology, Colloq. Math. Soc. Janos Bolyai
    36Ôćĺ**8** (1974), 243ÔÇô255.
    37Ôćĺ-/

    39Ôćĺopen SimpleGraph Cardinal
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺnamespace Erdos594
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ/-- A graph has uncountable chromatic number: it cannot be properly colored
    44Ôćĺwith countably many colors. -/
    45Ôćĺdef HasUncountableChromaticNumber {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
    46Ôćĺ  ÔłÇ (╬▒ : Type*) [Countable ╬▒], IsEmpty (G.Coloring ╬▒)
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ/-- The cycle graph $C_m$ on $m$ vertices ($m \geq 3$). Vertex $i$ is adjacent to vertex
    49Ôćĺ$i + 1 \pmod{m}$ and vertex $i - 1 \pmod{m}$. -/
    50Ôćĺdef cycleGraph (m : ÔäĽ) (_ : m Ôëą 3) : SimpleGraph (Fin m) where
    51Ôćĺ  Adj i j := i Ôëá j Ôłž (j.val = (i.val + 1) % m ÔłĘ i.val = (j.val + 1) % m)
    52Ôćĺ  symm := fun _ _ ÔčĘhne, hÔčę => ÔčĘhne.symm, h.elim Or.inr Or.inlÔčę
    53Ôćĺ  loopless := ÔčĘfun _ ÔčĘh, _Ôčę => h rflÔčę

    59Ôćĺ

/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software

WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 594

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/594](https://www.erdosproblems.com/594)

A problem of Erd┼Ĺs and Hajnal: does every graph with chromatic number
$\geq \aleph_1$ contain all sufficiently large odd cycles? Proved by
Erd┼Ĺs, Hajnal, and Shelah [EHS74].

[ErHa66] Erd┼Ĺs, P. and Hajnal, A., *On chromatic number of graphs and
set-systems*, Acta Math. Acad. Sci. Hung. **17** (1966), 61ÔÇô99.

[Er69b] Erd┼Ĺs, P., *Problems and results in combinatorial analysis and
graph theory*, Proof Techniques in Graph Theory (1969), 27ÔÇô35.

[EHS74] Erd┼Ĺs, P., Hajnal, A., and Shelah, S., *On some general properties
of chromatic numbers*, Topics in Topology, Colloq. Math. Soc. Janos Bolyai
**8** (1974), 243ÔÇô255.
-/

open SimpleGraph Cardinal

namespace Erdos594

/-- A graph has uncountable chromatic number: it cannot be properly colored
with countably many colors. -/
def HasUncountableChromaticNumber {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
  ÔłÇ (╬▒ : Type*) [Countable ╬▒], IsEmpty (G.Coloring ╬▒)

/-- The cycle graph $C_m$ on $m$ vertices ($m \geq 3$). Vertex $i$ is adjacent to vertex
$i + 1 \pmod{m}$ and vertex $i - 1 \pmod{m}$. -/
def cycleGraph (m : ÔäĽ) (_ : m Ôëą 3) : SimpleGraph (Fin m) where
  Adj i j := i Ôëá j Ôłž (j.val = (i.val + 1) % m ÔłĘ i.val = (j.val + 1) % m)
  symm := fun _ _ ÔčĘhne, hÔčę => ÔčĘhne.symm, h.elim Or.inr Or.inlÔčę
  loopless := ÔčĘfun _ ÔčĘh, _Ôčę => h rflÔčę

/-- $G$ contains a copy of $H$: there is an injective map preserving adjacency. -/
def SimpleGraph.ContainsCopy {V W : Type*}
    (G : SimpleGraph V) (H : SimpleGraph W) : Prop :=
  Ôłâ f : W Ôćĺ V, Function.Injective f Ôłž ÔłÇ u v, H.Adj u v Ôćĺ G.Adj (f u) (f v)

/--
Erd┼Ĺs Problem 594 [ErHa66] [Er69b]:

Every graph with uncountable chromatic number (i.e., chromatic number
$\geq \aleph_1$) contains all sufficiently large odd cycles. That is, there exists
$N_0$ such that for all odd $n \geq N_0$ (with $n \geq 3$), the graph contains $C_n$
as a subgraph.

Proved by Erd┼Ĺs, Hajnal, and Shelah [EHS74].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_594 {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V)
    (hG : HasUncountableChromaticNumber G) :
    Ôłâ NÔéÇ : ÔäĽ, ÔłÇ (n : ÔäĽ) (hn : n Ôëą 3), Odd n Ôćĺ n Ôëą NÔéÇ Ôćĺ
      G.ContainsCopy (cycleGraph n hn) := by
  sorry

end Erdos594

content.text: The review failed. The issue is that the problem is a yes/no question ("Does every graph...") so it needs the `answer(True) Ôćö P` form, with quantifiers moved inside the RHS of the `Ôćö`.

input.old_string:
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_594 {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V)
    (hG : HasUncountableChromaticNumber G) :
    Ôłâ NÔéÇ : ÔäĽ, ÔłÇ (n : ÔäĽ) (hn : n Ôëą 3), Odd n Ôćĺ n Ôëą NÔéÇ Ôćĺ
      G.ContainsCopy (cycleGraph n hn) := by
  sorry

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 594

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/594](https://www.erdosproblems.com/594)

A problem of Erd┼Ĺs and Hajnal: does every graph with chromatic number
$\geq \aleph_1$ contain all sufficiently large odd cycles? Proved by
Erd┼Ĺs, Hajnal, and Shelah [EHS74].

[ErHa66] Erd┼Ĺs, P. and Hajnal, A., *On chromatic number of graphs and
set-systems*, Acta Math. Acad. Sci. Hung. **17** (1966), 61ÔÇô99.

[Er69b] Erd┼Ĺs, P., *Problems and results in combinatorial analysis and
graph theory*, Proof Techniques in Graph Theory (1969), 27ÔÇô35.

[EHS74] Erd┼Ĺs, P., Hajnal, A., and Shelah, S., *On some general properties
of chromatic numbers*, Topics in Topology, Colloq. Math. Soc. Janos Bolyai
**8** (1974), 243ÔÇô255.
-/

open SimpleGraph Cardinal

namespace Erdos594

/-- A graph has uncountable chromatic number: it cannot be properly colored
with countably many colors. -/
def HasUncountableChromaticNumber {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
  ÔłÇ (╬▒ : Type*) [Countable ╬▒], IsEmpty (G.Coloring ╬▒)

/-- The cycle graph $C_m$ on $m$ vertices ($m \geq 3$). Vertex $i$ is adjacent to vertex
$i + 1 \pmod{m}$ and vertex $i - 1 \pmod{m}$. -/
def cycleGraph (m : ÔäĽ) (_ : m Ôëą 3) : SimpleGraph (Fin m) where
  Adj i j := i Ôëá j Ôłž (j.val = (i.val + 1) % m ÔłĘ i.val = (j.val + 1) % m)
  symm := fun _ _ ÔčĘhne, hÔčę => ÔčĘhne.symm, h.elim Or.inr Or.inlÔčę
  loopless := ÔčĘfun _ ÔčĘh, _Ôčę => h rflÔčę

/-- $G$ contains a copy of $H$: there is an injective map preserving adjacency. -/
def SimpleGraph.ContainsCopy {V W : Type*}
    (G : SimpleGraph V) (H : SimpleGraph W) : Prop :=
  Ôłâ f : W Ôćĺ V, Function.Injective f Ôłž ÔłÇ u v, H.Adj u v Ôćĺ G.Adj (f u) (f v)

/--
Erd┼Ĺs Problem 594 [ErHa66] [Er69b]:

Every graph with uncountable chromatic number (i.e., chromatic number
$\geq \aleph_1$) contains all sufficiently large odd cycles. That is, there exists
$N_0$ such that for all odd $n \geq N_0$ (with $n \geq 3$), the graph contains $C_n$
as a subgraph.

Proved by Erd┼Ĺs, Hajnal, and Shelah [EHS74].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_594 {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V)
    (hG : HasUncountableChromaticNumber G) :
    Ôłâ NÔéÇ : ÔäĽ, ÔłÇ (n : ÔäĽ) (hn : n Ôëą 3), Odd n Ôćĺ n Ôëą NÔéÇ Ôćĺ
      G.ContainsCopy (cycleGraph n hn) := by
  sorry

end Erdos594

+      Ôłâ NÔéÇ : ÔäĽ, ÔłÇ (n : ÔäĽ) (hn : n Ôëą 3), Odd n Ôćĺ n Ôëą NÔéÇ Ôćĺ
